text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, there was a young girl named Lily who loved collecting feathers. She lived far away from anywhere nice feathered birds collected. One day, while exploring an ancient forest edge where the trees were tall and twisted together, Lily happened upon a creature unlike any she’d ever seen.

It was a night owl. Her curious eyes took in its glossy black feathers, keen gray eyes, and huge pale owl feathers atop its head. But there was something unmistakably human about its face.

The night owl blinked at her, its voice a gentle hoot. “Well met.”

Startled, she shook off what surely was just a very clever owl costume.

“You’ve traveled far,” said the owl. “That’s quite an accomplishment.”

“I collect feathers. I think your feathers would look amazing.”

The creature chuckled, a deep barROW. “You’d take good care of them?”

“I would!”

With that tiny agreement, an odd alliance formed. Lily took vows to protect the owl
